name: GraphQL allows aliased query amplification
description: Sends a query that aliases the same field N times. GraphQL servers without a per-query alias-cap or query-complexity limit execute every alias independently — N requests' worth of work for one HTTP call. Combined with deep-nesting and/or batched queries this is a DoS amplifier; without batching it's still a CPU multiplier worth flagging because most GraphQL deployments don't gate aliases.

vulnerability.remediation: Configure a query-complexity limit or an explicit alias-count cap. HotChocolate: `.AddMaxAllowedFieldCycleDepth()` + `.AddMaxExecutionDepth()` and a custom IComplexityAnalyzerHandler that scores aliased fields. Apollo: `validationRules: [createComplexityRule({ maximumComplexity: 100 })]`. Verify by replaying the same probe and watching for an `errors` array with a complexity-limit message instead of N successful aliases.

steps.body: {"query":"{ a:__typename b:__typename c:__typename d:__typename e:__typename f:__typename g:__typename h:__typename i:__typename j:__typename }"}
